claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 11 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first check the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this, I need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask a few questions. Let me start with the first one:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! Now I need to understand a bit more about each of your children.

How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 21, Blake is 15, and Charlie is 11.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about Alex specifically, as they are over 20 years old.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training anymore. They're working full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements with the children.

Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, only Charlie lives with me. Alex and Blake don't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand more about Alex and Blake since they don't live with you.

Are you paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after Alex or Blake - for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to Alex's upkeep - I help with their living costs and expenses. But for Blake, I only give a bit of money here and there, nothing major really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify a few more details.

For Blake specifically - approximately how much do you contribute to Blake per month, and what does that cover (e.g., food, clothes, pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: For Blake, I give about £10 a week - it's just a bit of pocket money really, nothing more than that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand Blake's situation a bit better.

Where is Blake living, and who is the main person responsible for looking after Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with my ex-partner, who is the main person looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about Alex's situation to properly assess eligibility.

Is your ex-partner claiming Child Benefit for Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, my ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that clarification. Now regarding Alex - you mentioned you contribute to Alex's living costs and expenses. Can you tell me approximately how much you contribute per month or per week for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £50 a week towards Alex's upkeep - I help with their rent, food, and other living expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just to confirm a few final details:

Does Charlie, who lives with you, have any special circumstances - such as being in hospital for extended periods, in care, or working 24 hours or more a week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Charlie just lives at home with me and goes to school like a normal 11-year-old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also check - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit:

**Charlie (11 years old): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is under 16, lives with you, and nobody else is claiming for them. You meet all the eligibility criteria.

**Blake (15 years old): ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. Although Blake is under 16, your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Additionally, the £10 per week you contribute is less than the Child Benefit amount required to qualify when a child doesn't live with you.

**Alex (21 years old): ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and working full-time (not in education or training), they don't meet the age eligibility criteria.

**Summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Charlie. I recommend you apply as soon as possible, as you cannot backdate claims for more than 3 months.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 and working full-time, they do not meet the age eligibility criteria for Child Benefit, regardless of the financial contributions being made.

Blake is 15 years old, which meets the age criteria. However, Blake does not live with you, and the contribution of £10 per week is less than the Child Benefit amount (currently £25.60 for the eldest child or £16.95 for additional children). To qualify when a child doesn't live with you, you must contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. Additionally, your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.

Charlie is 11 years old, lives with you, attends school, has no special circumstances affecting eligibility, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You are clearly responsible for Charlie as you live together. This meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (21), Blake (15), and Charlie (11). Only Charlie is eligible for Child Benefit as they live with the claimant and meet all eligibility criteria. Alex is not eligible due to being over 20 and not in approved education or training. Blake is not eligible because another person (the ex-partner) is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim for the same child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 and working full-time, they do not meet the age eligibility criteria for Child Benefit, regardless of the financial contributions being made.

Blake is 15 years old, which meets the age criteria. However, Blake does not live with you, and the contribution of £10 per week is less than the Child Benefit amount (currently £25.60 for the eldest child or £16.95 for additional children). To qualify when a child doesn't live with you, you must contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. Additionally, your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.

Charlie is 11 years old, lives with you, attends school, has no special circumstances affecting eligibility, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You are clearly responsible for Charlie as you live together. This meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (21), Blake (15), and Charlie (11). Only Charlie is eligible for Child Benefit as they live with the claimant and meet all eligibility criteria. Alex is not eligible due to being over 20 and not in approved education or training. Blake is not eligible because another person (the ex-partner) is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim for the same child.
